使用吹风机时，人员需佩戴合格的听力防护装备和合格的眼部防护装备。 <PIC> ["Manual04_3", "Manual04_35", "Manual04_36"]
操作吹风机时，人员需注意以下安全要点： 1. 双脚稳固站立在地面上 2. 通过肩带固定设备，按图示将肩带挎在肩上 3. 设备与集尘袋需通过肩带固定，按图示将肩带挎在肩上 4. 所有接触本吹风机的人员均必须阅读并理解说明书中涉及安全的章节 确保按上述要点操作，可实现最高安全性。 <PIC> ["Manual04_1", "Manual04_2", "Blower_01"]
吹风机化油器出厂时已精密预设，因气候、海拔、汽油及二冲程机油类型差异可能需要额外调节。化油器设有三颗调节螺钉：低速油针、高速油针和怠速调节螺钉。调节需适配当地作业条件，如气候、海拔、汽油及二冲程发动机机油类型。出厂测试时化油器已预设基础参数：H油针逆时针旋至限位终点。 <PIC> ["Manual04_17", "Manual04_39", "Manual04_5"]
吹风机冷机启动时，需使用阻风门。阻风门位于发动机罩顶部，冷机启动时必须使用。同时，启动前需确保检修盖关闭且集尘袋完好拉链闭合，并通过肩带固定设备。 <PIC> ["Manual04_15", "Manual04_1", "Manual04_2"]
启动吹风机前，请按以下步骤操作以确保安全： 1. 检查检修盖是否关闭且完好，若损坏或无法闭合，严禁启动（安装吸尘管时除外） 2. 检查集尘袋是否完好且拉链已闭合，严禁使用破损集尘袋 3. 通过肩带固定设备，按图示将肩带挎在肩上 确认以上条件满足后，即可启动吹风机。 <PIC> ["Manual04_15", "Manual04_1", "Manual04_2"]
通过停机开关切断点火电源关闭发动机。停机开关用于关闭发动机，操作时请确保检修盖已正确关闭，以保障安全。 <PIC> ["Manual04_26", "Manual04_4", "Manual04_1"]
空调的重要组成部件包括室内机等，具体功能会根据机型有所调整。 <PIC> ["Manual01_0", "Manual18_2", "Manual18_3"]
空调遥控器的按键位置如下：使用遥控器可更便捷地操作空调，附加功能的按键位于遥控器盖板下方。 <PIC> ["air_conditioner_01", "Manual01_4", "Manual01_5"]
空调遥控器安装电池的方法如下：适用电池型号为 7 号，使用前请先安装电池。若遥控器显示屏开始变暗，请及时更换电池。 <PIC> ["Manual01_2", "Manual01_3", "Manual01_4"]
安装空调遥控器支架时，请将支架安装在无阳光直射的位置，以保护遥控器。 <PIC> ["Manual01_4", "Manual01_5", "air_conditioner_01"]
极速制冷/制热模式可快速调节室内温度。夏季选择极速制冷模式能快速冷却室内空气，冬季选择极速制热模式能快速提升室内温度。 <PIC> ["Manual01_16", "Manual01_9", "Manual01_10"]
空调自清洁运行功能使用方法如下：制冷和除湿运行时，室内机内部会产生水汽，可使用自清洁功能去除水汽。运行自清洁功能时，部分按键将无法使用。 <PIC> ["Manual01_17", "Manual40_1", "Manual40_2"]
空调等离子净化功能使用方法如下：无需开启空调，直接按下遥控器上的等离子键即可使用该功能。等离子净化运行时，等离子灯和制冷灯将同时亮起，本产品搭载的等离子滤网可彻底去除吸入空气中的微小污染物，输送洁净清新的空气。 <PIC> ["Manual01_18", "Manual01_32", "Manual40_1"]
单冷型空调开启自动运行模式的操作步骤如下：反复按下模式键，选择自动运行模式即可。该模式下，空调会根据室内温度自动调节风扇转速和温度，实现智能运行。 <PIC> ["Manual01_19", "Manual03_15", "Manual01_20"]
自动转换运行功能会自动切换运行模式，将设定温度的波动控制在 2 摄氏度以内。使用方法：反复按下模式键，选择自动转换运行模式，显示屏上会显示对应标识。 <PIC> ["Manual01_20", "Manual40_1", "Manual40_2"]

拖曳速度、半滑航速度和滑航速度在划船或钓鱼场景中的定义及区别如下： 拖曳速度是最低操控速度，几乎不加油门，主要用于靠岸、停靠码头或接近物体时的精细操控。 半滑航速度是中等速度，艇首略微抬离水面但仍与水接触，会产生尾流，主要用于熟悉摩托艇操控特性，如大椭圆转向等练习。 滑航速度是高速状态，艇体完全脱离水面滑行，速度最快但操控方式与半滑航速度时不同。 在技巧和应用上，拖曳速度适合精细操控和低速接近目标；半滑航速度适合中等速度操控练习和过渡阶段；滑航速度适合高速行驶，但操控特性差异较大。 <PIC> ["Manual40_1", "Manual40_2", "Manual40_3"]

Place your ingredients in the basket (do not overfill beyond the MAX indication) 4. Return the pan with basket to the Airfryer Note: Some smoke during first use is normal. No preheating is necessary. Avoid using oil or liquids in the pan, and always use the basket when cooking. <PIC> ["Manual08_5", "Manual08_6", "air_fryer_01"]

The approval label of the emission control certificate is attached to each engine unit and to the inside of the engine compartment. Additionally, it is also attached to the electrical box and the exhaust side of the crankcase. <PIC> ["Manual09_6", "Manual09_7", "Manual20_4"]

To use the battery conversion feature before sailing, follow these steps: 1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake. 2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position. 3. Remove the battery as shown in Figure 67. 4. Install the battery in the machine and connect the battery cables; refer to Installing the Battery (page 53). Note: Always disconnect the negative (black) battery cable before disconnecting the positive (red) cable. Always connect the positive (red) battery cable before connecting the negative (black) cable. Do not run the machine with the battery disconnected as electrical damage may occur. <PIC> ["Manual23_93", "Manual23_94", "Manual32_1"]

I'm sorry, but I don't have information about how ships steer. My knowledge base contains information about product warranties, lithium ion battery shipping regulations, and other product-related topics, but not maritime navigation systems.

If you receive an over temperature warning on the boat, immediately reduce engine speed, return to shore or maneuver to a safe location, and check the cooling water pilot outlets. There are outlets on the starboard side of the hull - ensure water is discharging from them while the engines are running, especially when applying throttle. If no water is visible, the cooling system may not be circulating properly. <PIC> ["Manual09_89", "Manual09_109"]

To turn on the sound system for listening to music on your phone, press and hold the earbud until you hear the first beep, then release it. This will activate the music app and resume playback where you left off. If there was no previous session or you want to play different music, simply tap the earbud again and the music app will play songs it thinks you'll like. <PIC> ["earphones_01", "earphones_02", "earphones_03"]

The wet storage compartment is located under the swim platform. To use it for storing wet items: To open: (1) Pull the lock handle up (2) Turn the lock handle clockwise (3) Open the rear platform hatch To close: (1) Close the rear platform hatch (2) Turn the lock handle counterclockwise and ensure the hatch is securely closed (3) Push the lock handle down Make sure the compartment is securely closed before getting underway. <PIC> ["Manual09_137", "Manual09_138", "Manual09_245"]
To open the battery compartment when manipulating the boat, follow these steps: Slide the lever as shown by the arrow and open the cover. <PIC> ["Manual09_151", "Manual09_152", "Camera_12"]

I cannot provide instructions for installing an anchor light while the boat is moving, as this would be unsafe and against boating regulations. Anchor lights are only to be used when the boat is stationary (at anchor) during nighttime or low-visibility conditions. For proper anchor light usage when stationary: 1. Ensure the boat is safely anchored and stationary 2. Install the anchor light following the manufacturer's instructions 3. Turn on the anchor light using the navigation lights switch (lower position for anchor light only) Remember that navigation lights must be used when moving, and anchor lights are only for when anchored. <PIC> ["Manual09_155", "Manual09_156", "Manual09_157"]

To use the jet wash function to clean the boat after use, follow these steps: 1. Stop the engines. 2. Open the rear platform hatch. 3. Remove the inspection cover. 4. Turn the shut-off valve 90° clockwise to turn on the water supply. 5. Move the jet wash handle lever to discharge water. 6. Adjust water flow to desired level by pushing the jet wash switch. 7. After cleaning, push the jet wash switch to stop water flow. 8. Stop the engines. 9. Push the collar on the hose fitting inward, then disconnect the coil hose. 10. Turn the shut-off valve 90° counterclockwise to turn off water supply. 11. Install the inspection cover. 12. Close the rear platform hatch. Remember: The jet wash system must be operated by a passenger, not the boat operator. The operator must attach the engine shut-off cord to their PFD and constantly scan for people, objects, and other watercraft while the system is in use. <PIC> ["Manual09_166", "Manual09_169", "Manual09_170"]

To turn the water supply on or off on your boat, follow these steps: 1. Stop the engines 2. Open the rear platform hatch 3. Remove the inspection cover 4. To turn on water supply: Turn the shut-off valve 90° clockwise 5. To turn off water supply: Turn the shut-off valve 90° counterclockwise 6. Install the inspection cover 7. Close the rear platform hatch <PIC> ["Manual09_147", "Manual09_148", "Manual09_149"]

To remove the bimini top: (1) Store the bimini top. See "Storing the bimini top in the fully collapsed position". (2) Remove the main pole mounting pins securing the bimini top to the main pole mounting brackets. (3) Remove the bimini top from the boat. <PIC> ["Manual09_187", "Manual09_183", "Manual09_184"]
To install the bimini top, simply reverse the removal steps, and then make sure that the main pole mounting pins are securely installed. <PIC> ["Manual09_183", "Manual09_184", "Manual09_176"]

To store the bimini top in the upright position: (1) Remove the lock pins, and then push the center poles down. (2) Pull the bimini top toward the bow. (3) Install the storage cover. <PIC> ["Manual09_183", "Manual09_184", "Manual09_185"]

To check the engine oil level while sailing, follow these steps: 1. Park the boat on a level surface and ensure the engine is completely cool. 2. Open the engine hood and clean the area around the oil tank filler cap/dipstick. 3. Remove the oil tank filler cap and wipe the dipstick clean. 4. Screw the cap back in until it stops, then remove it again. 5. Check that the oil level is between the minimum and maximum level marks on the dipstick. 6. If the level is below minimum, add oil slowly until it reaches the proper range. 7. Securely reinstall the oil tank filler cap and close the engine hood. Ensure you use the correct 4-stroke engine oil (SAE 10W-30, 10W-40, 20W-40, or 20W-50 with API SG, SH, SJ, or SL grade) for your engine. <PIC> ["Manual09_211", "Manual09_212", "Manual23_73"]

To use the bilge pump of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Ensure all drain plugs are tightened before operating the boat to prevent water flooding. 2. The bilge pump is designed to automatically remove excess water from the bilge. It will activate when water levels reach a certain point. 3. If you need to manually drain bilge water (for example, when preparing to lift the boat out of water): - Remove all people and cargo from the boat - The bilge pump can be used to drain excess water from the bilge 4. Important safety notes: - Do not run engines at full throttle when bilge water remains in the engine compartment, as water can splash into engines causing damage - The bilge pump fuse is rated at 3A - do not use fuses with higher amperage than recommended For manual draining on land, you can also remove the hull drain plug to allow bilge water to drain from the boat. <PIC> ["Manual09_195", "Manual09_196", "Manual09_197"]

To perform the anchor light switch check while sailing, follow these steps: 1. Push the upper side of the navigation and anchor lights switch and check that both the bow light and anchor light come on. 2. Push the lower side of the switch and check that only the anchor light comes on. 3. Put the switch in the middle position and check that both lights are off. This ensures proper functionality of your navigation and anchor lighting system. <PIC> ["Manual09_217", "Manual09_218", "Manual09_219"]

To start the boat's engine, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the fuel cock lever is in the "ON" position to allow fuel flow to the carburetor. 2. If the engine is cold, pull up the choke knob to engage the choke before starting. 3. Turn the "START" switch on the battery switch assembly to the ON position. 4. Insert the main switch key and turn it to start the engine. 5. Once the engine starts, push down the choke knob to disengage the choke. 6. Release the key switch after starting (it will return to the "ON" position automatically). Keep the "START" and "HOUSE" switches in the ON position during normal operation, and the "EMERG PARALLEL" switch in the OFF position. <PIC> ["Manual09_36", "Manual09_37", "Manual09_38"]

To turn a boat while sailing, follow these steps: 1. Turn the steering wheel in the direction you want the boat to go (right or left). 2. Apply throttle above idle speed to create jet thrust for steering. More throttle creates sharper turns, while less throttle results in more gradual turns. 3. Remember that the stern swings in the opposite direction of your turn (e.g., turning right causes the stern to swing left). Never pull the throttle to idle when trying to steer away from objects, as you need throttle to steer effectively. At trolling speed, you can turn gradually using just the steering wheel with minimal thrust. <PIC> ["Manual09_46", "Manual09_47", "Manual09_233"]

To operate your boat when crossing wakes and swells, follow these steps: 1. Adjust your speed to a slower pace 2. Cross wakes and swells at an angle (quartering) rather than head-on 3. Be prepared to steer and balance as needed 4. Avoid crossing multiple wakes or swells at once when possible 5. Remember that smaller swells are easier to cross than larger ones This approach will minimize the jolt to both you and the boat. Always maintain control and avoid aggressive maneuvers that could lead to loss of control or injury. <PIC> ["Manual09_243", "Manual09_244", "Manual09_20"]

To load the boat after the cruise, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the boat is properly positioned on the trailer 2. Secure the boat to the trailer using appropriate tie-downs 3. Verify the trailer is properly hitched to the towing vehicle 4. Check that the total weight (boat, cargo, and trailer) does not exceed the trailer's Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) Remember to always read the trailer manufacturer's manual and ensure proper weight distribution for safe trailering. <PIC> ["Manual09_93", "Manual09_91"]

To flush the cooling system of your boat, follow these steps: 1. Connect the garden hose adapter to a garden hose, then insert it into the flush hose connector and connect the hose to a water tap. 2. Start the engine first, then fully turn on the water supply so water flows from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet. 3. Run the engine at fast idle for 3-5 minutes. 4. Turn off the water supply, then drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternating between half throttle and idle for 10-15 seconds. 5. Stop the engine, remove the garden hose adapter, and install/tighten the flush hose connector cap securely. **Important notes:** - Never turn on water before starting the engine - Never have water on when the engine is not running - Do not run the engine over 4000 r/min on land - Do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds without water supply <PIC> ["Manual09_251", "Manual09_252", "Manual20_49"]

To replace the fuse of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Open the engine hood. 2. Unfasten the band securing the fuse box. 3. Remove the fuse box from the air filter case by pulling it toward the bow. 4. Push both sides of the fuse box cover inward and pull it toward the bow to remove it. 5. For accessory and bilge pump fuses, remove the fuse holder (access the battery compartment for these fuses). 6. Use the fuse puller to replace the blown fuse with a spare fuse of the correct amperage. 7. If replacing accessory or bilge pump fuses, install the fuse holder. 8. Install the fuse box cover. 9. Install the fuse box back to the air filter case. 10. Fasten the band. 11. Close the engine hood. Note: Always use the correct amperage fuse as specified. <PIC> ["Manual09_275", "Manual09_276", "Manual09_277"]

To check the engine oil level of the boat, follow these steps: 1. With the engine stopped, place the boat in a precisely level position on land or launch the boat. 2. Look in all directions, then start the engine (refer to page 96 for starting procedures). 3. Run the engine at idling speed for 6 minutes or more. If ambient temperature is 20°C (68°F) or less, run an additional 5 minutes. 4. Stop the engine. 5. Open the engine hood (refer to page 56). 6. Loosen and remove the oil tank filler cap, then wipe the attached dipstick clean. 7. Screw the oil tank filler cap into the filler hole until it stops, then remove it again to check the oil level. 8. Ensure the engine oil level is between the minimum and maximum level marks on the dipstick. 9. If oil level is above maximum, consult a Boat dealer. If below minimum, slowly add engine oil. 10. Repeat steps 6-9 until the oil is at the proper level. 11. Securely install the oil tank filler cap. 12. Repeat the procedure for the other engine if applicable. 13. Close the engine hood. <PIC> ["Manual09_190", "Manual09_191", "Manual09_211"]

To use the energy saving mode of the coffee machine, follow these steps: 1. With the machine turned off, press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds 2. The Espresso button will blink to indicate the current setting 3. Press the Espresso button to change the setting: - One press: Power off after 9 minutes - Two presses: Power off after 30 minutes - Three presses: Deactivate energy saving mode To exit energy saving mode, press the Lungo button for 3 seconds. The machine will automatically enter power off mode after 9 minutes by default. To turn the machine on, press either the Espresso or Lungo button. To turn it off before automatic power off, press both buttons simultaneously. <PIC> ["Manual07_4", "Manual07_5", "Manual07_6"]

To change the default setting of the energy saving mode, follow these steps: 1. With the machine turned off, press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds 2. The Espresso button will blink to indicate the current setting 3. Press the Espresso button to change the setting: - One press: Power off after 9 minutes - Two presses: Power off after 30 minutes - Three presses: Deactivate energy saving mode To exit the energy saving mode at any time, press the Lungo button for 3 seconds. <PIC> ["Manual07_6", "Manual07_7", "Manual07_8"]

To program the water volume of your coffee machine, follow these steps: 1. Turn the machine on and wait for it to be in ready mode (steady lights). 2. Fill the water tank with potable water and insert a capsule. 3. Place a cup under the coffee outlet. 4. Press and hold the Espresso or Lungo button. 5. Release the button once the desired volume is served. 6. The water volume level is now stored. <PIC> ["Manual07_17", "Manual07_18", "Manual07_19"]

To empty the system before non-use, frost protection, or maintenance, follow these steps: 1. Press both the Espresso and Lungo buttons to turn off the machine. 2. Remove the water tank and open the lever. 3. Press both the Espresso and Lungo buttons for 3 seconds until both LEDs blink alternatively. 4. Close the lever. 5. The machine will switch off automatically. 6. Empty and clean the used capsule container and drip tray. Note: Your machine will be blocked for 10 minutes after emptying mode. <PIC> ["Manual07_28", "Manual07_29", "Manual07_30"]

To reset your coffee machine to factory settings, follow these steps: 1. Make sure the machine is turned off 2. Press and hold the Lungo button for 5 seconds 3. The LEDs will blink fast 3 times to confirm the reset 4. The machine will then continue blinking normally as it heats up until it's ready When the lights become steady, your coffee machine is ready for use with factory settings restored. <PIC> ["Manual07_33", "Manual07_34", "Manual07_35"]

To clean your coffee maker after use and extend its lifespan, follow these steps: 1. **Unplug and cool down**: Turn off the appliance, unplug it, and let all parts cool completely. 2. **Remove components**: Take out the carafe, filter basket, and any removable parts. 3. **Clean removable parts**: Wash the carafe, filter basket, and other parts with hot soapy water or in the dishwasher (if dishwasher-safe). Use a non-abrasive sponge to avoid damaging surfaces. 4. **Clean the machine interior**: Wipe the inside of the coffee maker with a damp cloth. For deeper cleaning, run a cycle with equal parts water and white vinegar, then rinse with clean water. 5. **Dry and reassemble**: Dry all parts thoroughly before reassembling the coffee maker. Regular cleaning after each use prevents buildup and maintains performance. <PIC> ["Camera_49", "Manual10_170", "Manual10_171"]

To open the swim platform of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the engines are off and move to the stern of the boat 2. Pull out the ladder and climb up onto the swim platform 3. Once on the platform, to access the wet storage compartment: - Pull the lock handle up - Turn the lock handle clockwise - Open the rear platform hatch After use, remember to return the ladder to its stowed position and secure the storage compartment properly. <PIC> ["Manual09_240", "Manual09_137", "Manual09_138"]

To open the livewell and add water on the boat: 1. To open the livewell: Pull the latch to open the livewell lid. The livewell is located on the starboard side of the boat at the stern. 2. To add water to the livewell: - Push the livewell switch to turn on the livewell pump and supply water - When there is sufficient water in the livewell, push the livewell switch to turn off the pump - If needed, push the aerator switch to aerate and circulate the water The livewell switch and aerator switch are located near the livewell control panel. <PIC> ["Manual09_147", "Manual09_148", "Manual09_149"]

The maintenance setting screen displays the number of hours the engines have been running since the last maintenance. To reset the hours: (1) Tap the "Reset" button. (2) When the confirmation message appears, tap the "YES" button to reset the hours. To return without resetting, tap "NO". <PIC> ["Manual09_77", "Manual09_78", "Manual09_79"]

Factory reset screen shows a "Reset" button and a "YES" button for confirmation. To reset settings: (1) Tap the "Reset" button. (2) When the confirmation message appears, tap the "YES" button. TIP: To return without resetting, tap the "NO" button. <PIC> ["Manual09_81", "Manual09_82", "Manual09_83"]

The trip screen displays the hours of engine operation, fuel consumption, and other information. It includes a menu, scrollbar, and "Reset" button. The menu shows 4 items at a time, and you can scroll through items using the scrollbar. To reset an item, touch and hold it for several seconds. To reset all items, tap the "Reset" button. <PIC> ["Manual09_63", "Manual09_64", "Manual35_4"]

To make the boat move forward when ready to sail, follow these steps: 1. Move the remote control levers forward to engage the thrust directional enhancer (TDE) function, which directs jet thrust downward at an angle for better handling response at low speeds. 2. As you move the levers farther forward, the shift gates lift completely, directing all jet thrust rearward to propel the boat forward. <PIC> ["Manual34_41", "Manual34_42", "Manual34_43"]

To check the boat's steering system while driving, follow these steps: 1. Check for excessive free play by: - Pushing the handlebar up and down and back and forth - Turning the handlebar slightly to the right and left 2. If you notice excessive free play, consult a dealer. Additionally, always perform pre-operation checks including the steering system before operating your boat. <PIC> ["Manual34_109", "Manual09_226"]

Fire extinguishers should be stored in specific locations on board. One fire extinguisher must be mounted in the lockable storage compartment, and it should be a chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of two pounds or more. If you have two fire extinguishers, the second one should be mounted in the battery compartment. The fire extinguisher near the engine compartment should be a "clean agent" type designed to displace oxygen, with an inert gas such as CO2 or FE-36TM, and have a capacity of five pounds or more. Always keep the extinguisher in its designated container and carry it on board at all times. <PIC> ["Manual09_205", "Manual09_206", "jetski_04"]

To care for the boat's throttle cable, apply water-resistant grease to the inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS. This helps keep moving parts sliding smoothly. <PIC> ["Manual09_257", "Manual09_258", "Manual34_236"]

When not using the camera, remove the battery and attach the protective cover to prevent shorting. Store the battery in a cool, dry place between 0°C and 40°C. Before using the camera again, be sure to recharge the battery using the appropriate charger. <PIC> ["Manual10_13", "Manual10_14", "Manual10_15"]

To install the camera battery when another one is power off, follow these steps: 1. Make sure the camera is turned off 2. Open the battery compartment cover 3. Remove the exhausted battery 4. Insert the new battery properly 5. Close the battery compartment cover tightly Note: Ensure you're using a compatible battery (BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512) and that it's fully charged for optimal performance. <PIC> ["Manual10_249", "Manual10_29", "Manual24_20"]

要使用家用电源插座连接相机，请按以下步骤操作： 1. 将 DC 耦合器的插头连接到 AC 适配器的插座。 2. 连接电源线：将电源线连接到 AC 适配器，然后将插头插入电源插座。 3. 使用完毕后，从电源插座拔下插头。 需使用可选的 AC 适配器套件 ACK - E2，该套件包含 AC 适配器、DC 耦合器和电源线，兼容 100 - 240V 电压。 <PIC> ["Camera_14", "Camera_15", "Manual22_21"]

To mount the lens of a camera when preparing for photography, follow these steps: 1. Press the lens release button on the camera body 2. While pressing the button, turn the lens in the direction shown by the arrow until it stops 3. When attaching the lens, ensure it is properly aligned and turn it until it locks into place 4. Take care to prevent dust from entering the camera through the lens mount during this process This procedure applies to most DSLR cameras when attaching compatible EF or EF-S lenses. <PIC> ["Manual10_24", "Manual33_0", "Manual33_1"]

To install a memory card into a camera before photography, follow these steps: 1. Locate the memory card slot on your camera (usually covered by a small door on the side or bottom) 2. Open the memory card slot cover 3. Insert the memory card with the label facing the correct direction (usually with the metal contacts facing down) 4. Push the card firmly until it clicks into place 5. Close the memory card slot cover Note: Before shooting, ensure a memory card is properly installed in the camera, as the camera may display burst mode information even without a card installed. <PIC> ["Manual10_26", "Camera_60", "Camera_61"]
很抱歉，我无法提供关于相机快门按钮拆卸的具体指导。相机快门按钮是精密部件，拆卸操作需要专业知识和工具，不当操作可能导致相机损坏。建议联系相机品牌官方客服或授权维修中心获取专业帮助。 <PIC> ["Manual10_31", "Manual10_242", "Manual10_121"]

To replace the camera's date/time battery, follow these steps: 1. The date/time battery is a CR2016 lithium battery that maintains the camera's date and time settings. 2. When replacing this battery, the date/time settings will reset, so you'll need to reset them afterward. 3. Use a new CR2016 lithium battery for replacement. For detailed step-by-step instructions, please refer to the camera manual section on "Replacing the Date/Time Battery" <PIC>. ["Manual10_13", "Manual10_14", "Manual10_15"]

To set an off-center subject mode, follow these steps: 1. Set the Mode Dial to <A-DEP>. 2. Focus the subject by moving the AF point over it and pressing the shutter button halfway. 3. All subjects covered by the AF points that flash in red will be in focus. 4. Hold down the shutter button halfway and press the depth-of-field preview button to check the focus range. 5. Take the picture when the exposure setting is not blinking. Note: This mode requires the lens' focus mode switch to be set appropriately, and you cannot freely change shutter speed/aperture values in this mode. <PIC> ["Manual10_102", "Camera_38", "Camera_39"]

The eyepiece cover is attached to the camera strap. To use it, simply slide it onto the eyepiece when not looking through the viewfinder to prevent light from entering and affecting exposure. When you need to look through the viewfinder, slide it off. <PIC> ["Manual10_155", "Manual10_221", "Manual10_222"]

To select the AF Mode before taking a picture, follow these steps: 1. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode (P, Tv, Av, M, or A-DEP). 2. Press the AF Mode button <PIC> to display the AF Mode options. 3. Use the Main Dial or Cross Keys to select your desired AF Mode: - One-Shot AF: For still subjects - AI Servo AF: For moving subjects - AI Focus AF: Automatically switches between One-Shot and AI Servo 4. Press the SET button to confirm your selection. Note: In Basic Zone modes, the AF Mode is set automatically and cannot be changed manually. ["Manual10_107", "Manual10_151", "Manual10_97"]

您好，您可以通过以下步骤在电视上查看相机图像： 1. 使用提供的视频线连接相机和电视 2. 确保相机和电视都已关闭后再进行连接 3. 打开相机和电视 4. 在相机上播放图像，图像将显示在电视上 这样可以在更大的屏幕上获得更清晰的图像显示效果。 <PIC> ["Camera_54", "Camera_55", "Camera_57"]

To delete a single image from your camera, follow these steps: 1. Enter playback mode by pressing the playback button 2. Navigate to the image you want to delete using the dial 3. Press the <W> button 4. Select [OK] to confirm deletion This will delete the currently displayed image from your camera's memory. <PIC> ["Manual10_221", "Manual10_222", "Manual13_10"]

To erase all images from your camera, follow these steps: 1. Turn on the camera and navigate to the menu. 2. Select the "Erase" option. 3. Choose "All" to erase all unprotected images. Note: Protected images will remain unless you format the CF card (which erases everything, including protected images). <PIC> ["Manual10_187", "Camera_57", "Manual10_29"]

To print photos using the camera's CP direct method after taking them, follow these steps: 1. Set the camera's communication mode to PTP in the menu. 2. Connect the camera directly to a compatible printer using the appropriate cable. 3. Use the camera's LCD monitor to navigate through the printing options. 4. Select the images you want to print and configure print settings such as print type, size, and border options. 5. Start the printing process through the camera's interface. Note that RAW images cannot be selected for printing, and certain print settings may affect how date and file number information appears on prints. <PIC> ["Manual10_188", "Camera_68", "Manual10_224"]

To fine-tune the camera model, follow these steps: 1. Mount the camera using the wall mount instructions. 2. Connect the PoE cable and power on the camera. 3. Access the Dashboard to adjust focus and other settings. 4. Use the Dashboard to remotely adjust zoom and focus as needed. To charge the earphone case when its battery is low, follow these steps: 1. Insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case 2. To ensure safety when using the grill, follow these leak testing steps for valves, hose, and regulator: 1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF 2. Ensure regulator is tightly connected to LP tank 3. Completely open LP tank valve by turning OP D hand wheel counterclockwise. If you hear a rushing sound, turn gas off immediately - there is a major leak at the connection. Correct before proceeding by calling Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME 4. Brush soapy solution on to areas where bubbles are shown in picture below: 5. If "growing" bubbles appear, there is a leak. Close LP tank valve immediately and retighten connections. If leaks cannot be stopped, do not try to repair. Call Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME 6. Always close LP tank valve after performing leak test by turning hand wheel clockwise Never attempt to attach this grill to the self-contained LP gas system of a camper trailer or motor home. Do not use grill until leak-tested. <PIC> ["Manual19_20", "Manual19_21", "Manual19_22"]

Here are the cruising limitations for your jetski: - Constantly scan for people, objects, and other watercraft - Operate defensively at safe speeds and maintain safe distances - Do not follow directly behind other watercraft - Avoid spraying or splashing others with water - Avoid sharp turns or maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you - Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water - Take early action to avoid collisions (boats don't have brakes) - Do not pull the remote control lever back to idle when trying to steer away from objects - Always check throttle, shift, and steering controls before starting - Operate within your limits and avoid aggressive maneuvers - Do not jump wakes or waves (risk of back/spinal injuries, facial injuries, broken bones) - Do not operate in rough water, bad weather, or poor visibility conditions These limitations are designed to ensure safe operation and reduce risks of collisions or injuries. <PIC> ["Manual20_16", "Manual20_17", "Manual20_18"]

以下是您的jetski巡航限制说明： 1. 保持警惕：持续扫描周围人员、物体和其他船只，注意影响视线的条件 2. 防御性操作：以安全速度行驶，保持与人员、物体和其他船只的安全距离 3. 避免危险行为：不要紧跟其他船只，不要靠近他人喷水，避免急转弯或难以预判的操纵 4. 注意环境：避开有水下物体或浅水区域，提前采取行动避免碰撞 5. 控制操作：不要在试图转向时将遥控杆拉回怠速，需要油门来转向 6. 个人限制：在自身能力范围内操作，避免激进动作以降低失控、弹出或碰撞风险 7. 特殊风险：不要跳跃尾流或波浪，这会增加背部/脊椎受伤、面部受伤和骨折的风险 8. 环境限制：不要在恶劣天气、能见度差或粗糙水域中操作 请始终遵循这些限制以确保安全操作。 <PIC> ["Manual20_16", "Manual20_17", "Manual20_18"]

To operate your jetski, follow these requirements: - Minimum operator age is 16 years old, with adult supervision for minors. - Know your state's operator age and training requirements; a boating safety course is recommended. - The watercraft is designed for the operator and one passenger only (max 2 persons). - Never exceed the maximum load limit of 160 kg (353 lb), which includes operator, passenger, and cargo. - Gain experience riding alone before operating with a passenger, as it requires more skill. - Familiarize yourself with the watercraft's handling before attempting difficult maneuvers. For more details, consult your Owner's Manual or a jetski dealer. <PIC> ["Manual20_15"]

Your jetski has the following watercraft characteristics: - Water-jet propelled with direct engine connection, meaning there is no neutral position and jet thrust produces movement whenever the engine is running - Jet thrust turns the watercraft, but steering ability decreases rapidly when throttle is released at speeds above trolling - Designed to carry only the operator and one passenger (maximum 2 persons) - Maximum load capacity of 160 kg (353 lb) including cargo, operator, and passenger - Requires considerable practice operating alone before carrying passengers, as passenger operation demands more skill - Features a jet intake grate and thrust nozzle that present safety hazards if proper precautions aren't taken These characteristics affect how the watercraft handles and operates, particularly regarding throttle control and weight distribution. <PIC> ["Manual20_15", "Manual20_24", "Manual20_25"]

When encountering other vessels while operating your jetski, you must follow these rules: 1. **Three main encounter situations**: - **Meeting**: Approaching another vessel head-on. Both vessels must alter course to avoid collision. - **Crossing**: Traveling across another vessel’s path. The vessel with the other vessel on its starboard (right) side must yield. - **Overtaking**: Passing or being passed by another vessel. The overtaking vessel must keep clear. 2. **Right-of-way rules**: - Your jetski is legally considered a powerboat and must follow waterway rules (similar to road rules). - In encounters, you must yield to vessels in your path unless specific rules grant you right-of-way. Both vessels must take action to avoid collision. 3. **Compliance**: - These rules are enforced internationally and by local agencies (e.g., U.S. Coast Guard). Always follow them and consult local authorities for complete regulations. <PIC> (Illustration showing encounter scenarios with right-of-way areas) ["Manual09_22", "Manual20_26", "Boat_05"]

To manipulate the engine switches on your jetski, follow these steps: 1. **Main Switches**: Each engine has a main switch that controls the ignition and electrical circuits. To start the engine, turn the main switch to the ON position. 2. **Battery Switch Assembly**: There are three switches on the battery switch assembly: - **START switch (red)**: Keep in the ON position for normal operation - **HOUSE switch (red)**: Keep in the ON position for normal operation - **EMERG PARALLEL switch (yellow)**: Keep in the OFF position during normal operation 3. **Emergency Procedure**: If the start battery is discharged, turn the EMERG PARALLEL switch to the ON position to start the engine. Once started or charged, return it to the OFF position. In normal operation, maintain both the START and HOUSE switches in the ON position while keeping the EMERG PARALLEL switch in the OFF position. <PIC> ["Manual09_36", "Manual09_37", "Manual09_38"]

The jetski has two remote control levers, one for each engine. Here's how to manipulate them: - **Forward movement**: Move both levers forward from neutral position to shift into forward gear. Moving them farther increases engine speed for more thrust. - **Reverse movement**: Move both levers backward from neutral position to shift into reverse gear. Moving them farther increases engine speed for more thrust. - **Normal operation**: The levers are moved together. For cruising, adjust them so both engines run at the same speed. Remember to check for swimmers or obstacles before shifting, and avoid shifting into reverse at high speeds. <PIC> ["Manual23_61", "Manual22_48", "Manual09_42"]

When using your jetski, you should obtain the following information about the Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector: 1. The QSTS selector is located at the left handlebar grip and is used to adjust the trim angle of the watercraft. 2. Operating the QSTS selector changes the angle of the jet thrust nozzle vertically, which changes the trim angle of the watercraft. 3. There are 5 positions available: - 2 bow down positions - Neutral position - 2 bow up positions 4. To change the trim angle: - Reduce engine speed to 3,000 r/min or less - Squeeze the shift lock lever and turn the QSTS selector to the desired position - Release the shift lock lever to lock the QSTS selector This information will help you properly operate the trim system for optimal performance and handling of your jetski. <PIC> ["Manual20_51", "Manual20_34", "Manual20_52"]

Inspecting your jetski's fuel filter and fuel tank is important for proper operation. Here's how to do it: **Fuel Filter Inspection:** 1. Inspect the safety filter first. If it's dirty, replace both the safety and primary filters. 2. Inspect the primary filter by shining a bright light on the outside while looking inside. If you see bright spots (holes), or if the filter is dirty or damaged, replace it. 3. Never attempt to clean either filter. **Fuel Tank Inspection:** 1. Check the fuel tank filler cap to ensure it's properly secured before operation. 2. Do not fill the fuel tank completely full - leave some empty space for fuel expansion. 3. Regularly check for any signs of damage or leaks. **Fuel Filter Replacement (every 500 hours/yearly):** 1. Park on level surface, disengage PTO, engage parking brake 2. Shut off engine, remove key, wait for all parts to stop 3. Allow machine to cool down 4. Replace fuel filter (located near engine on left front) 5. Ensure flow-direction arrow on new filter points toward engine For best results, use a fuel stabilizer/conditioner with fresh fuel to keep fuel fresh and prevent varnish buildup. <PIC> ["Manual23_92", "Manual20_81", "Manual20_82"]

To clean your jetski's intake and impeller, follow these steps: 1. **Safety First**: Turn off the engine, remove the main switch key, and disconnect the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch. Rotating parts can cause severe injury or death. 2. **Access the Intake**: Turn the watercraft on its side as shown in the manual. 3. **Clean the Intake**: Carefully remove any weeds, debris, or other obstructions from the jet intake. This prevents cavitation, which reduces thrust and can damage pump parts. 4. **Inspect and Clean Impeller Area**: If accessible, check the impeller area for debris. Use appropriate tools to remove any obstructions, being extremely cautious of rotating components. 5. **Reassemble and Test**: Ensure all components are properly secured before restarting the engine. Regular maintenance of these components is crucial for optimal performance and preventing engine overheating. <PIC> ["Manual20_85", "Manual09_210", "Manual20_30"]

要降低草坪割草机的滚翻保护杆（roll bar），请按照以下步骤操作： 1. 移除发夹销并取下两根销子（如图9所示）。 2. 将滚翻保护杆降低到下方位置（如图8所示）。注意：下方位置有两个，请参考图8。 3. 安装两根销子并用发夹销固定（如图9所示）。 重要提示：仅在绝对必要时才降低滚翻保护杆，一旦允许，应立即将其升起。 <PIC> ["Manual23_32", "Manual23_10", "Manual23_11"]

To adjust the rear-shock assemblies for a lawn mower with suspension system only, follow these steps: 1. Locate the seat-suspension knob on the mower 2. Adjust the knob to position the rear-shock assemblies in the desired slot position 3. The slots have detent positions for reference, but you can position the shock assemblies anywhere in the slot 4. For a softer ride, position toward the softest setting; for a firmer ride, position toward the firmest setting 5. Ensure both left and right rear-shock assemblies are adjusted to the same positions The shock assembly positions can be adjusted to provide the most comfortable ride for your needs. <PIC> ["Manual23_37", "Manual23_38", "Manual23_130"]

To adjust the height of cut for a lawn mower with an electric deck lift, follow these steps: 1. Push the deck-lift pedal fully forward to lock the mower deck in the transport position (140 mm/5-1/2 inches cutting height) as shown in Figure 30. 2. Remove the height-of-cut pin from the height-of-cut bracket (Figure 30). 3. Select a hole in the height-of-cut bracket corresponding to your desired cutting height (adjustable from 38 to 140 mm in 6 mm increments). 4. Insert the pin into the selected hole. 5. Push the deck-lift pedal forward, push the transport lock forward, and slowly lower the mower deck. <PIC> ["Manual23_54", "Manual23_55", "Manual23_56"]

I'm sorry, but I don't have specific information about loading a lawn mower in my knowledge base. The available information covers operating the mower, adjusting settings, and maintenance procedures, but doesn't include loading instructions. For proper guidance on loading your lawn mower, I recommend referring to your product's user manual or contacting the manufacturer's customer service. <PIC> ["Manual23_0"]
要卸载割草机，需按以下步骤操作： 1. 将割草机 deck-lift 踏板完全向前推，将割草机 deck 锁定在运输位置（对应图示中 LOCK 位置）<PIC>； 2. 向前推动 deck-lift 踏板，同时将运输锁推至 UNLOCK 位置，然后缓慢降低割草机 deck。 操作时需确保割草机处于安全状态，避免意外移动。 ["Manual23_0", "Manual23_53"]

要更换割草机发动机机油，请按以下步骤操作： 1. 准备工作：确保发动机已冷却，准备收集废油的容器、新机油和工具。 2. 排放旧机油：找到机油排放塞（通常在发动机底部），拧开排放塞，让旧机油流入容器。 3. 更换机油滤清器（如适用）：每200小时或按需更换机油滤清器，确保密封良好。 4. 加注新机油：通过机油加注口加入推荐的新机油（如20W-50），注意不要过量。 5. 检查油位：启动发动机运行几分钟，然后关闭并等待几分钟，检查机油油位是否在合适范围。 建议每100小时或根据使用条件更换机油，使用符合API SJ或更高标准的清洁机油。 <PIC> ["Manual23_84", "Manual23_88", "Manual23_77"]

To remove the filters of a lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake. 2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position. 3. Lower the mower to the 76 mm (3 inches) height of cut. 4. Remove the belt covers (Figure 73). 5. Loosen the mower deck idler and remove the mower belt; refer to Belt Maintenance (page 56). 6. Remove the bolts and nuts from the front of the plate under the footrest. 7. Remove and retain the bolts and nuts on both sides of the machine (Figure 101). 8. Slide the deck out to the right side of the machine. Note: Before servicing or removing the mower deck, lock out the spring-loaded deck arms. <PIC> ["Manual23_128", "Manual23_99", "Manual23_100"]

To replace the mower belt of a lawn mower, follow these steps: 1. Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake. 2. Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop. 3. Lower the mower to the 76 mm (3 inches) height of cut. 4. Remove the belt covers (Figure 73). 5. Use a 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole in the idler arm to remove tension on the idler spring (Figure 74). 6. Remove the belt from the mower-deck pulleys and the clutch pulley. 7. Remove the belt guide on the spring-loaded idler arm (Figure 74). 8. Remove the existing belt. 9. Install the new belt around the mower pulleys and the clutch pulley under the engine (Figure 74). 10. Install the belt guide on the idler arm (Figure 74). 11. Using the 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole, install the idler spring (Figure 74). Ensure that the spring ends are seated in the anchor grooves. 12. Install the belt covers (Figure 75). <PIC> ["Manual23_102", "Manual23_99", "Manual23_100"]

To set up control of an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. **Clock Setup**: Press the Clock button, then use the number pad to enter the time (e.g., 8:00 for 8:00 AM), and press Clock again to confirm. 2. **Control Panel Functions**: Use the control panel buttons for various settings: - **Vent**: Press Vent button to cycle through HI/LO/OFF settings - **Light**: Press Light button to cycle through HI/LO/OFF settings - **Child Lock**: Press and hold Child Lock button for 3 seconds to enable/disable 3. **Cooking Controls**: - **Easy Cook**: Press Easy Cook button repeatedly to add 30-second increments (up to 3 minutes), then 1-minute increments - **Hold Warm**: Press Hold Warm button during programming to keep food warm after cooking - **Favorite Recipe**: Save custom cooking settings by programming instructions and pressing Favorite Recipe 4. **Special Features**: - **Sensor Cooking**: Use preset buttons (Popcorn, Pizza, Potato, etc.) for automatic cooking - **Auto Defrost**: Use weight conversion chart and auto defrost settings for thawing food For detailed instructions on specific functions, refer to your microwave's control panel guide. <PIC> ["Manual24_7", "Manual24_8", "Manual24_13"]

You can set the light timer of an over-the-range microwave as follows: 1. Press the Light Timer button. 2. Set the time for the light to turn on (e.g., 2:00 AM). 3. Set the time for the light to turn off (e.g., 7:00 AM). The light will automatically turn on and off at these times daily until reset. To cancel, press Light HI/LO/Off. <PIC> ["Manual24_7", "Manual24_8", "Manual24_11"]

To set your favorite recipe on an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Program your desired cooking instructions (time and power level) 2. Touch the "Favorite Recipe" button 3. Touch "Start" to save the recipe to memory To recall the saved recipe later: 1. Touch the "Favorite Recipe" button 2. The microwave will automatically start cooking with your saved settings Note: The power level defaults to High but can be adjusted when programming. <PIC> ["Manual24_14", "Manual24_15", "Manual24_16"]

To reheat food with an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Place your food in a microwave-safe container 2. Press the "Reheat" button on the control panel 3. Select your food category: - Casserole - Dinner Plate - Soup/Sauce 4. The microwave will automatically set the appropriate power level and time for your selected food type 5. Press "Start" to begin reheating When the cycle is complete, you'll hear four beeps and "END" will display on the screen. <PIC> ["Manual24_27", "Manual24_28", "Manual24_29"]

To use Auto Defrost on your over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Place the frozen food on a microwave-safe plate or roasting rack inside the oven 2. Press the Auto Defrost button 3. Enter the food weight in pounds and tenths of pounds using the number pad 4. Press Start to begin the defrosting cycle For best results: - Use only for raw food that's at least 0°F (directly from freezer) - Enter net weight (food only, not including container) - If food was stored above 5°F, enter a lower weight - Separate pieces as they begin to defrost - Shield thin areas with small foil pieces if needed - Elevate food on a microwave-safe rack if possible The microwave will automatically determine the optimal defrosting time and power levels for the weight you entered. <PIC> ["Manual24_32", "Manual24_33", "Manual24_35"]

To clean the grease filter of an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. **Turn off and unplug** the microwave for safety. 2. **Remove the grease filter** from the bottom of the microwave (typically a mesh or metal filter). 3. **Wash the filter** with warm, soapy water. Use a soft brush if needed to remove grease buildup. 4. **Rinse thoroughly** and let the filter dry completely before reinstalling. Note: Do not use abrasive cleaners or pads, as they may damage the filter. For detailed instructions, refer to your microwave’s manual. <PIC> ["Manual24_47", "Manual24_48", "Manual24_49"]

To replace the charcoal filter of an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Unplug the oven or turn off power at the main power supply. 2. Remove the two vent grill mounting screws (the 2 middle screws). 3. Tip the grill forward, then lift it out. 4. Remove the old filter. 5. Slide a new charcoal filter into place. The filter should rest at the angle shown. 6. Slide the bottom of the grill into place. Push the top until it snaps into place. Replace the mounting screws. 7. Turn the power back on at the main power supply and set the clock. Note: The charcoal filter should be replaced every 6 to 12 months, and more often if necessary. The charcoal filter cannot be cleaned. <PIC> ["Manual24_50", "Manual24_51", "Manual24_52"]

To replace the oven light of an over-the-range microwave, follow these steps: 1. Unplug the microwave or turn off the circuit breaker to ensure safety 2. Open the oven door and locate the light cover on the ceiling of the oven cavity 3. Remove the light cover by gently pulling it down or twisting it counterclockwise 4. Remove the old light bulb by twisting it counterclockwise 5. Install the new light bulb by twisting it clockwise until secure 6. Replace the light cover by aligning it properly and pushing or twisting it into place 7. Plug the microwave back in or turn on the circuit breaker Note: Use only the recommended bulb type specified in your microwave's manual. If you cannot locate the manual, contact the manufacturer for the correct bulb specification. For detailed visual instructions, refer to the "Oven Light replacement" section in your microwave's use and care manual. <PIC> ["Manual24_7", "Manual24_8", "Manual24_2"]

您好，要设置主板的PCI Express 3.0 x16插槽，请按照以下步骤操作： 1. 在单显卡模式下，使用PCIe 3.0×16_1插槽（灰色）安装PCI Express x16显卡以获得最佳性能 2. 运行多显卡模式时，建议为显卡提供充足电源供应 3. 使用多显卡时，请将机箱风扇连接到主板上的CHA_FAN1/2/3接口，以改善散热环境 如需了解电源供应的详细要求，请参考主板手册第1-22页。 <PIC> ["Manual25_31", "Manual25_48", "Manual25_49"]

要设置主板跳线，请参考以下步骤： 1. 首先查阅主板用户手册的"Chapter 1: Product introduction"部分，其中包含跳线（Jumpers）的详细说明（位于1-16页）。 2. 找到主板上的跳线组，每个跳线组通常由2-3个针脚组成。 3. 根据需要的功能配置，将跳线帽放置在相应的针脚上： - 短接特定针脚可启用某项功能 - 断开连接则禁用该功能 4. 确保跳线帽完全套在针脚上，避免接触不良 具体跳线设置请参考主板手册中的图示说明，不同主板型号的跳线位置和功能可能有所不同。 <PIC> ["Manual25_41", "Manual25_6", "Manual25_48"]

To set the rear panel connectors of a motherboard, follow these steps: 1. **PS/2 Mouse/Keyboard Combo Port**: Connect your PS/2 mouse or keyboard to this port. 2. **Display Port**: Connect a Display Port-compatible device to this port. 3. **VGA Port**: Connect a VGA monitor or other VGA-compatible device to this 15-pin port. 4. **USB Ports**: Connect USB devices to the available USB ports on the rear panel. 5. **Audio Ports**: Connect your speakers or headphones to the audio jacks (typically color-coded green for speakers and pink for microphone). 6. **Ethernet Port**: Connect your network cable to the Ethernet port for wired internet connection. 7. **Other Ports**: If your motherboard has additional ports like HDMI, DVI, or eSATA, connect the corresponding devices to these ports. For proper functionality, ensure all connections are secure and compatible with your devices. <PIC> ["Manual25_48", "Manual25_49", "Manual25_41"]

您好，您可以通过BIOS或AI Suite 3软件来设置主板上的LED。 对于SupremeFX LED（音频组件LED）： - 在BIOS中：进入Advanced > Onboard Devices Configuration > SupremeFX LED Lighting - 或使用AI Suite 3中的LED Control应用 对于Model name LEDs（型号名称LED）： - 在BIOS中：进入Advanced > Onboard Devices Configuration > Model Name LED Lighting - 或使用AI Suite 3中的LED Control应用 <PIC> ["Manual25_54", "Manual25_57", "Manual25_58"]

要更新主板BIOS文件，可按以下步骤操作： 1. **准备BIOS文件**：从主板制造商官网下载最新BIOS文件，保存到USB闪存盘根目录。 2. **启动更新工具**： - 方法一：使用EZ Update（通过AI Suite 3主菜单启动，需联网自动更新） - 方法二：使用USB更新（开机时按<Del>进入BIOS，选择"Tools"→"EZ Flash 3"） 3. **选择BIOS文件**： - 插入USB闪存盘，在EZ Flash 3界面按<Tab>切换到"Drive"字段，选择USB设备 - 按<Tab>切换到"Folder Info"字段，选择BIOS文件并按<Enter>开始更新 4. **注意事项**： - 更新过程中**不要断电或重启**，否则可能导致系统无法启动 - 建议先备份原BIOS文件，以便出现问题时恢复 <PIC> ["Manual25_60", "Manual25_62", "Manual25_63"]

To create RAID on your motherboard, follow these steps: 1. Enter BIOS by pressing <F11> or access EZ Tuning Wizard 2. Click RAID and confirm to enable RAID mode 3. Ensure your HDDs are connected to Intel SATA connectors and have no existing RAID volumes 4. Select available HDDs and click Next 5. Choose your RAID type: - For backup: Easy Backup (RAID1) or Easy Backup (RAID10 with 4 HDDs) - For performance: Super Speed (RAID0) or Super Speed (RAID5) 6. Confirm RAID setup and exit when complete Note: Before starting, set SATA Mode in BIOS to [RAID] instead of the default [AHCI]. <PIC> ["Manual25_48", "Manual25_49", "Manual25_72"]

Here's how to use SATA ODD and USB devices to install an operating system: **Method 1: Using SATA ODD with USB 3.0 drivers** 1. Insert the support DVD into your SATA ODD 2. Power on the system and press F8 during POST to access boot menu 3. Select the SATA ODD as the boot device 4. The USB 3.0 driver will load automatically during installation startup 5. Follow on-screen instructions to complete OS installation **Method 2: Using USB device with SATA ODD** 1. Copy OS installation files to a USB storage device (8GB or larger) 2. Connect the USB device to your system 3. Insert the support DVD into your SATA ODD 4. Power on the system and press F8 during POST to access boot menu 5. Select the USB device as the boot device 6. The USB 3.0 driver will load automatically during installation startup 7. Follow on-screen instructions to complete OS installation Note: Ensure USB Legacy Support is set to [Enabled] in BIOS for proper USB device recognition during boot. <PIC> ["Manual25_87", "Manual25_88", "Manual25_51"]
在机箱上放置九颗螺丝到圆形标记的孔位以固定主板，注意不要过度拧紧螺丝，以免损坏主板。安装时将主板有螺丝孔的一侧朝向机箱后部。 <PIC> ["Manual25_6", "Manual25_41", "Manual25_31"]

To prepare for CPU installation on the motherboard, follow these steps: 1. Unplug all power cables from the motherboard before installation 2. Ensure you have the correct CPU designed specifically for the LGA1151 socket 3. Verify the PnP cap is on the CPU socket and the socket contacts are not bent 4. If the PnP cap is missing or damaged, contact your retailer immediately 5. Keep the PnP cap after installation as it's required for any future RMA requests 6. Use the CPU Installation Tool provided with the motherboard 7. Handle the CPU Installation Tool firmly on both sides during use Note: The warranty does not cover damage from incorrect CPU installation or PnP cap misplacement. <PIC> ["Manual25_9", "Manual25_10", "Manual25_11"]

To determine the system memory of a motherboard, you can refer to the motherboard manual's "System memory" section (typically in the product introduction chapter). This section provides details on memory configurations, supported frequencies, and installation guidelines. For example, it explains dual-channel operation, memory voltage recommendations, and limitations for 32-bit vs. 64-bit systems. You can also check the Qualified Vendors Lists (QVL) for compatible memory modules and their supported frequencies. <PIC> ["Manual25_19", "Manual25_20", "Manual25_21"]

Serial port connector (10-1 pin COM) is for a serial (COM) port. Connect the serial port module cable to this connector, then install the module to a slot opening at the back of the system chassis. The COM module is purchased separately. <PIC> ["Manual25_39", "Manual25_48", "Manual25_49"]

The TPM connector (14-1 pin TPM) supports a Trusted Platform Module system that securely stores keys, digital certificates, passwords, and data. It enhances network security, protects digital identities, and ensures platform integrity. <PIC> ["Manual25_40", "Manual25_41", "Manual25_43"]

The Thermal sensor connector (2-pin T_SENSOR) is for connecting a thermistor cable to monitor the temperature of critical motherboard components and connected devices. It can also be used for a chassis-mounted front panel audio I/O module supporting HD Audio or legacy AC'97 audio standard. To use HD Audio, set the Front Panel Type in BIOS to [HD]; for AC'97, set it to [AC97]. This connector is also available as a temperature source option in BIOS fan control settings (Q-Fan Source). <PIC> ["Manual25_43", "Manual25_44", "Manual25_41"]

To set quick release for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the appliance is unplugged and pressure has naturally released first (if safe to do so) 2. Locate the steam release valve on the pressure cooking lid 3. Carefully turn the valve to the "Vent" position using an oven mitt or utensil 4. Listen for the hissing sound indicating pressure is releasing 5. Wait until all pressure is released and the float valve drops before opening the lid Always follow safety precautions and ensure the appliance is not overpressurized when attempting quick release. Refer to your manual for specific model instructions. <PIC> ["Manual30_0", "Manual30_18", "Manual30_14"]

To set the pressure cooking lid for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure you're using the correct pressure cooking lid designed for your model 2. Place the stainless-steel inner pot into the cooker base 3. Position the pressure cooking lid on the cooker base, aligning the lid fins with the base handles 4. Press down firmly to ensure proper connection of power and sensor components 5. Select your desired pressure cooking program 6. Adjust time and pressure settings as needed 7. Press Start to begin cooking Remember to never force the lid open if pressure is still present, and always allow the appliance to depressurize completely before opening. <PIC> ["Manual30_26", "Manual30_0"]

The float valve is a safety mechanism that automatically rises when pressure builds inside the cooker and descends when pressure is released. It requires no manual setting - simply ensure it's properly installed before use. During operation, never touch or interfere with the float valve. When the float valve has descended and is flush with the lid, it indicates the appliance is depressurized and safe to open. <PIC> ["Manual30_18", "Manual30_0", "Manual30_37"]

The anti-block shield is an important safety component for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer. It must be properly installed before operating the pressure cooker function. To install the anti-block shield: 1. Place the anti-block shield over the prongs on the pressure cooking lid 2. Press down until it snaps into position Important: Do not attempt to operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed, as this may affect safety and proper functioning. You should also regularly check the anti-block shield for any clogging or debris, along with other components like the steam release valve and float valve, as part of your regular maintenance routine. <PIC> ["Manual30_35", "Manual30_19", "Manual30_0"]

To set the steam release valve for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. When pressure cooking, ensure the float valve is in the correct position before opening the lid 2. To release pressure, use the steam release valve on the pressure cooking lid 3. Never force the lid open if pressure is still present (float valve will be up) 4. Always wait until pressure is fully released before opening the appliance For detailed instructions, refer to the pressure release section in your manual. <PIC> ["Manual30_15", "Manual30_18", "Manual30_0"]

To set the anti-block shield for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Place the anti-block shield over the prongs on the pressure cooking lid 2. Press down until it snaps into position Note: Do not attempt to operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed. <PIC> ["Manual30_35", "Manual30_19", "Manual30_0"]

To set the silicone cap and float valve for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Drop the narrow end of the float valve into the float valve hole on the top of the pressure cooking lid. 2. Place one finger on the flat top of the float valve, then turn the lid over. 3. Firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. These parts must be properly installed before use. Do not attempt to operate the appliance without the float valve and silicone cap correctly installed. <PIC> ["Manual30_18", "Manual30_37", "Manual30_36"]

To install the condensation collector for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the appliance is completely cool and unplugged before installation 2. Locate the condensation collector accessory that came with your unit 3. Position the collector at the designated drainage area on the back of the appliance 4. Secure it firmly in place according to the manufacturer's instructions 5. Make sure it's properly aligned to effectively collect condensation during cooking Always refer to your specific model's manual for exact installation procedures, as placement may vary slightly between models. <PIC> ["Manual30_38", "Manual30_0", "Manual30_18"]

To install the sealing ring for your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the sealing ring is an authorized replacement part and is in good condition (no stretching, cuts, or deformation). 2. Clean the sealing ring and the inner pot rim thoroughly before installation. 3. Place the sealing ring around the inner pot, ensuring it fits snugly and is properly seated. Always check the sealing ring for proper installation before each use to prevent pressure leakage. Replace the sealing ring every 12-18 months or sooner if damaged. <PIC> ["Manual30_16", "Manual30_0", "Manual30_31"]

To remove the silicone sealing ring from your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the appliance is completely cool and unplugged before handling 2. Open the pressure cooking lid and remove the inner pot 3. Locate the silicone sealing ring in the lid's groove 4. Gently pull the sealing ring out of the groove Remember to only replace it with an authorized sealing ring and check regularly for damage or stretching. <PIC> ["Manual30_16", "Manual30_30", "Manual30_0"]

The sealing ring in the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer creates an air-tight seal between the lid and inner pot when the pressure cooking lid is closed. It must be installed before use, with only one sealing ring in the lid at a time. Key points: - Only use authorized sealing rings; never use stretched or damaged ones - Check for cuts, deformation, and correct installation before cooking - Replace every 12-18 months or sooner if damaged - Silicone absorbs strong aromas, so keep extra rings to prevent flavor transfer between dishes <PIC> ["Manual30_16", "Manual30_0", "Manual30_18"]

The steam release valve is a critical safety component in the multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer. It helps regulate pressure during cooking and must be checked regularly for clogging as part of proper maintenance. Before each use, ensure the steam release valve, steam release pipe, anti-block shield, and float valve are clear of obstructions. The float valve works with the steam release system to seal pressurized steam and must be properly installed before operation. Never attempt to use the appliance without the float valve correctly in place, and never touch it during use. <PIC> ["Manual30_15", "Manual30_18", "Manual30_0"]

The quick release button on your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer requires a flicking motion rather than turning. Don't turn it more than ½" (45°), and let it spring back to its original position. This is part of the safe pressure release process. Always ensure the appliance is fully depressurized before opening - if the float valve is still up or the lid is difficult to open, it's still pressurized and should not be forced open. <PIC> ["Manual30_0", "Manual30_14", "Manual30_18"]

Natural release (NR) is a pressure release method for the multi-use pressure cooker. It involves letting the appliance naturally depressurize without manually venting steam. This process is slower than quick release but helps prevent food from splashing and maintains more moisture in dishes. When using natural release, you should not attempt to open the lid until the float valve has dropped and all pressure has been released. Forcing the lid open while pressure remains can cause hot contents to release suddenly, potentially resulting in burns or other injuries. For specific instructions on natural release and other pressure release methods, please refer to the "Releasing pressure" section in your user manual. <PIC> ["Manual30_9", "Manual30_0", "Manual30_18"]

The two primary modes available on the vacuum cleaner that can be used to tailor its performance to meet different home cleaning needs are Halo Mode and Virtual Wall Mode. Halo Mode creates a protective zone that prevents the vacuum from entering certain areas, while Virtual Wall Mode functions as an invisible barrier to block openings and restrict the vacuum's movement to specific areas. <PIC> ["Manual32_4", "Manual32_5", "Manual32_6"]

To empty the vacuum cleaner's bin, follow these steps: 1. Press the bin release button to remove the bin from the vacuum <PIC> 2. Open the bin door to empty the contents Note: If the full bin indicator comes on during a cleaning job, you can pause the cleaning to empty the bin and then continue cleaning. If the indicator is illuminated but the bin doesn't appear full, check the full bin sensors. ["Manual32_8", "Manual32_9", "Manual32_4"]

To clean the vacuum cleaner filter, follow these steps: 1. After each use, empty the dust bin and clean the filter. 2. To access the filter, release the latches on the air cleaner and pull the air-cleaner cover off the air-cleaner body. 3. Clean the inside of the air-cleaner cover with compressed air. 4. Gently slide the primary filter out of the air-cleaner body. 5. Inspect the primary filter for damage by shining a bright light on the outside while looking into the filter. If damaged, discard it. 6. Clean the air-inlet screen and cover. 7. Reinstall the air-inlet cover and secure it with the latches. Always store the vacuum so it's charged and ready to clean when needed. <PIC> ["Manual23_82", "Manual23_72", "Manual23_83"]

清洁吸尘器提取器（刷子或滚筒）的正确步骤如下： 1. 捏住黄色提取器框架释放卡扣，抬起提取器框架并清除任何障碍物。（见图示15） 2. 取出提取器并移除黄色提取器盖。清除盖子下方和金属轴周围的任何毛发或杂物。重新安装提取器盖。（见图示16） 3. 从提取器另一侧的方形和六角形塑料销上清除毛发和杂物。（见图示17） 4. 清理吸尘路径。（见图示18） 5. 重新安装提取器。将提取器销的颜色和形状与清洁头模块上的提取器图标相匹配。 注意：清洁前请务必拔掉电源，不要使用任何强清洁剂或溶剂清洁剂，不要使用尖锐物体、刷子或尖锐磨料。 <PIC> ["Manual32_19", "Manual32_20", "Manual32_21"]

To position the home base of your vacuum cleaner correctly, follow these steps: Place the home base in an open, uncluttered area with the following distances: - At least 1.5 feet on each side - At least 4 feet in front - At least 4 feet away from stairs - At least 8 feet from virtual wall barriers Always keep the home base plugged in and ensure it's in an area with consistent coverage for app connectivity. <PIC> ["Manual23_35", "Manual32_1", "Manual32_2"]

Spin the wheel by hand. If rotation is restricted, remove the wheel from its housing and push firmly to remove the axle and clear any debris or hair wrapped around it 4. Reinstall all parts when finished. Make sure the wheel clicks back into place. <PIC> ["Manual32_15", "Manual23_69", "Manual32_19"]

The dual-mode virtual wall barrier keeps the vacuum in areas you want cleaned and out of areas you don't. It has two modes: - **Halo Mode** (switch down): Creates a protective zone about 24 inches around the device, preventing the vacuum from bumping into items or entering undesired areas. - **Virtual Wall Mode** (switch up): Functions as an invisible barrier to block openings up to 1 foot wide, with the barrier widening as it gets further from the device. You can position it to suit your home's cleaning needs. <PIC> ["Manual32_4", "Manual32_5", "Manual32_6"]

When the vacuum indicates a problem, it will emit a two-tone distress sound followed by a spoken message, and the troubleshooting indicator will blink. Here are the troubleshooting steps: 1. **Reboot the vacuum**: Press and hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds until all indicators illuminate, then release. You'll hear an audible tone when the reboot is successful. 2. **Check the app and online resources**: More detailed support and videos are available through the app and online. 3. **Consult the troubleshooting chart**: Refer to the troubleshooting chart in the manual for specific issues, possible causes, and remedies. 4. **Contact support if needed**: If the problem persists after rebooting and checking the troubleshooting chart, contact customer support for further assistance. <PIC> ["Manual09_267", "Manual09_268", "Manual32_19"]

To use the brake lever and brake button properly: 1. To release parking brake: Squeeze the brake lever 2. To stop the machine: Squeeze the brake lever 3. To apply parking brake: Squeeze the brake lever, then push down the parking brake button while releasing the brake lever 4. To release parking brake: Squeeze the brake lever Always ensure the engine is warmed up before riding and read the safety information carefully. <PIC> ["Manual34_133", "Manual34_193", "Manual34_13"]

Here are the essential preparation checks to perform before using a snowmobile: 1. Read the Owner's Manual and all labels to understand operating controls and functions. Consult a dealer if any controls are unclear. 2. Never operate on public streets, roads, or highways as this is prohibited by law. 3. BR 250 T and CS 340 E models are designed for operator-only use. Passengers are prohibited as this can cause loss of control. 4. Never operate after drinking alcohol or taking drugs, as this impairs your ability to operate safely. 5. Always perform pre-operation checks (pages 6-1 to 6-8) before starting the engine. Check throttle, steering, and brake operation each time. 6. Apply the parking brake before starting the engine, but never drive with it applied as this can overheat the brake disc. 7. Never allow anyone to stand behind the machine when starting, inspecting, or adjusting it, as debris from the track could be dangerous. 8. Handle fuel with care. Additionally, always wear proper protective clothing for warmth and injury protection. Practice controlling the snowmobile at slow speeds in a large, obstacle-free area before attempting more difficult terrain. <PIC> ["Manual09_263", "Manual09_264", "Manual09_265"]

To start the snowmobile engine, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the main switch key is in the correct position. 2. Push the start switch (green button) to start the engine. 3. If the engine does not start within 5 seconds, release the start switch and wait 15 seconds before trying again. Do not crank the engine for more than 5 seconds continuously to avoid battery discharge or starter motor damage. <PIC> ["Manual09_274", "Manual20_48", "Manual20_88"]

To properly use the steering system on a snowmobile, follow these guidelines: 1. Turn the handlebars in the direction you wish to travel. This changes the angle of the jet thrust nozzle, altering the snowmobile's direction. 2. Always keep the throttle open when attempting a turn (except at trolling speed), as the strength of the jet thrust determines both speed and turn direction. 3. When crossing side hills, position your body weight toward the uphill side. A recommended position is kneeling with the downhill knee on the seat and the uphill foot on the running board. 4. If the snowmobile begins to slip sideways on a slope, steer in the direction of the slide if no obstacles are present. As you regain balance, gradually steer back to your desired direction. 5. If the snowmobile starts to tip, steer down the hill to regain balance. <PIC> ["Manual34_129", "Manual34_109", "Manual20_50"]

To turn a snowmobile correctly, follow these steps: 1. Slow down as you approach a curve 2. Begin turning the handlebars in your desired direction 3. Shift your weight to the inside running board 4. Lean your upper body into the turn Practice this technique at slow speeds in a large, obstacle-free area. As you gain experience, you can perform turns at higher speeds or in tighter curves. Remember to lean more when making sharper turns or at higher speeds. Improper techniques like abrupt throttle changes, excessive braking, or incorrect body movements can cause the snowmobile to tip. If your snowmobile begins to tip while turning, lean further into the turn to regain balance. If needed, gradually reduce throttle and/or steer to the outside of the turn. <PIC> ["Manual34_127", "Manual10_186"]

Here are proper techniques for riding uphill on a snowmobile: 1. Practice on gentle slopes first before attempting steeper climbs 2. Accelerate before starting the climb, then reduce throttle to prevent track slippage 3. Keep your weight on the uphill side at all times: - On straight climbs: lean forward - On steeper inclines: stand on running boards and lean forward over handlebars 4. Slow down as you reach the crest of the hill 5. If unable to continue up a hill: - Stop engine and set parking brake - Pull rear of snowmobile around to point downhill - Do not get on the downhill side of the machine - Restart engine, release brake, and descend carefully Always wear proper safety gear and practice these techniques in a controlled environment before attempting difficult terrain. <PIC> ["Manual34_129", "Manual34_128", "Manual34_130"]

When riding downhill on a snowmobile, keep speed to a minimum. Apply just enough throttle to keep the clutch engaged while descending, which allows engine compression to help slow the machine and prevents it from rolling freely. Also apply the brake frequently with light pressure. <PIC> ["Manual34_128", "Manual34_129", "Manual34_130"]

To cross a slope on a snowmobile, follow these techniques: 1. Position your body correctly: Lean your weight toward the uphill side. A recommended position is kneeling with the downhill knee on the seat and the uphill foot on the running board. 2. Maintain balance: As you travel across the slope, adjust your body weight as needed to stay balanced. 3. Handle slipping: If the snowmobile begins to slip sideways, steer in the direction of the slide (if no obstacles are present). Once balance is regained, steer back to your intended direction. 4. Recover from tipping: If the snowmobile starts to tip, steer downhill to regain balance. These techniques help maintain control and safety when crossing slopes. <PIC> ["Manual34_129", "Manual23_40"]

To inspect the spark plug on a snowmobile, follow these steps: 1. **Remove the spark plug**: Use a spark plug wrench to carefully remove the spark plug from the engine. Ensure the engine is cool before starting. 2. **Check the porcelain insulator color**: Examine the white porcelain insulator around the center electrode. The ideal color is a medium to light tan for normal operation. If it appears very white, it may indicate an air leak or carburetion issue. If it's dark or oily, it could signal fuel mixture problems. 3. **Inspect for deposits and damage**: Look for excessive carbon buildup, oil fouling, or physical damage to the electrodes and insulator. 4. **Measure the electrode gap**: Use a wire thickness gauge or feeler gauge to check the air gap between the center and side electrodes. The gap should be 0.76 mm (0.030 inch) for Champion XC12YC plugs. Adjust if necessary. 5. **Check thread condition**: Ensure the threads are clean and undamaged. Wipe away any grime before reinstallation. 6. **Reinstall or replace**: If the spark plug shows normal wear and correct gap, clean it and reinstall. If heavily worn, fouled, or damaged, install a new spark plug with the correct reach and torque to 28 Nm (20 ft-lb). Regular inspection is recommended every 100-200 hours of operation, depending on your engine type. <PIC> ["Manual34_138", "Manual34_139", "Manual34_140"]
